_meta.description: Algorithmic shapes present in the CodeBite catalog, indexed by tag. Each shape lists worlds (from worlds.json) where the shape lands naturally, with a one-line hint on how it maps. Use during a rewrite to pick a world whose vocabulary hosts the shape without contortion.
_meta.usage: Given a problem's tag and the rough algorithmic move it makes, find the matching sub_shape, pick one of its worlds, and write the rewrite in that world's vocabulary.

sub_shapes.summary: Locate two elements (by index) whose values sum to a target.

worlds.hint: Two shelves whose combined widget count equals an order size.
Two items whose prices sum to a coupon threshold.
Two tier prices that together match a spending budget.

sub_shapes.summary: Detect whether any value repeats in a collection.

worlds.hint: Two copies with identical call numbers on the same shelf.
Two headlines with identical slugs in the ingest buffer.
Two parcels with the same tracking code scanned in one bag.

sub_shapes.summary: Test whether two sequences use the same multiset of tokens.

worlds.hint: Two recipes that call for the same multiset of ingredients.
Two bales whose material mixes are permutations of each other.
Two shot lists that use the same set of props in different order.

sub_shapes.summary: Bucket items by a canonical signature (e.g. sorted characters).

worlds.hint: Cluster articles by normalized topic fingerprint.
Group books by a normalized-title signature.
Group barrels by their tasting-note signature.

sub_shapes.summary: Return the k items with highest occurrence count.

worlds.hint: The k most common destination zone codes in a shift.
The k most frequently requested targets in a week.
The k most booked machines today.

sub_shapes.summary: For each index, compute an aggregate over all other elements (e.g. product).

worlds.hint: Station throughput as the product of all other stations' rates.
Per-plot flow share excluding that plot's own draw.
Per-station anomaly contribution excluding self.

sub_shapes.summary: Longest run of consecutive integers present in a set.

worlds.hint: Longest run of consecutive call numbers present on a shelf.
Longest block of consecutive seat numbers still available.
Longest run of consecutive bin numbers fully stocked.

sub_shapes.summary: Verify each row/column/block of a grid obeys a uniqueness or sum rule.

worlds.hint: Each block (row/col/region) must include each grape variety exactly once.
Each row of tanks must host each species exactly once.

sub_shapes.summary: Converging pointers to test mirror equality.

worlds.hint: A call-number string that reads the same forward and backward.
A shot sequence whose opening and closing shots mirror each other.

sub_shapes.summary: Two-pointer scan on a sorted array to hit a target sum.

worlds.hint: Two shelves on a sorted rack whose stock sums to an order.
Two trays on a sorted bake roster whose combined count matches demand.

sub_shapes.summary: Fix one index, two-pointer the rest for a three-element target.

worlds.hint: Three calls whose priority deltas cancel out.
Three stations whose cycle-time surpluses and deficits sum to zero.

sub_shapes.summary: Pick two index positions to maximize a width × min-height area.

worlds.hint: Two reservoir walls framing the largest capacity pool.
Two partition walls framing the largest bale footprint.

sub_shapes.summary: Two-pointer in-place dedup or shift.

worlds.hint: Collapse duplicate barcodes in the scanned list in place.
Push unscannable pieces to the end of a tray without reallocating.

sub_shapes.summary: One pass keeping running min and best spread.

worlds.hint: Best drain/fill gap over a moisture-reading series.
Biggest temperature swing from a day's low to a later high.

sub_shapes.summary: Variable-size window expanding while tokens stay unique.

worlds.hint: Longest run of headlines without repeating a topic.
Longest conveyor stretch without a material repeat.

sub_shapes.summary: Shrink-and-grow window that must cover a required multiset.

worlds.hint: Shortest shelf span covering every required section.
Shortest baking run covering every required recipe.

sub_shapes.summary: Fixed-size window with running max / sum / count.

worlds.hint: Max event rate in any 10-minute sliding window.
Max flow across any contiguous 5-intersection stretch.

sub_shapes.summary: Variable window where at most k edits/replacements/violations allowed.

worlds.hint: Longest production run tolerating at most k defects.
Longest block of seats tolerating at most k holds.

sub_shapes.summary: Push opening, pop on matching close; fail fast.

worlds.hint: Nested print-job groups must open and close in order.
Scene-take markers must nest (slate/cut) correctly.

sub_shapes.summary: Operand-stack evaluation of postfix tokens.

worlds.hint: Compose bale weights using postfix merge operations.
Fold severity ops given in postfix order.

sub_shapes.summary: Monotonic stack to resolve next-greater/smaller per index.

worlds.hint: Days until a warmer reading per station.
Nearest upcoming tier priced higher than this one.

sub_shapes.summary: Stack-driven expansion of nested repeat groups.

worlds.hint: Expand a compressed assembly plan with nested k(x) repeats.
Expand a compressed route spec like 3(A-B-C).

sub_shapes.summary: Stack that answers min in O(1) via an auxiliary stack.

worlds.hint: Active-patient stack that reports lowest acuity on top in O(1).

sub_shapes.summary: Binary search on an array rotated once.

sub_shapes.summary: Binary search to find the pivot in a rotated array.

worlds.hint: Identify the line's terminus in a rotated station list.

sub_shapes.summary: Binary search the answer on a monotone feasibility predicate.

worlds.hint: Minimum flow rate that drains all reservoirs before deadline.
Minimum conveyor speed that clears the day's material by close.

sub_shapes.summary: Binary search across a row- and column-sorted grid.

worlds.hint: Find a flow value in a row/column sorted intersection-density matrix.

sub_shapes.summary: Reverse pointer direction through a singly linked chain.

worlds.hint: Reverse the order of stops in a route chain.

sub_shapes.summary: Floyd's tortoise-and-hare or hashset over chain nodes.

worlds.hint: Detect whether a line's transfer chain loops back on itself.
Detect a forwarding loop in a redirect chain.

sub_shapes.summary: Interleave two sorted chains into one sorted chain.

Merge two wards' patient queues (each already acuity-sorted).

sub_shapes.summary: Two-pointer skip to remove the k-th node from tail.

sub_shapes.summary: Recursive or stack-based DFS over a rooted tree.

Walk the region→city→postcode tree top-down.

sub_shapes.summary: BFS over a rooted tree emitting nodes by depth.

List assembly→subassembly→part by depth level.

sub_shapes.summary: Swap left and right children recursively.

worlds.hint: Mirror a scene-breakdown tree for a reverse-chronology edit.
Mirror a blending tree to test symmetric flavor profiles.

sub_shapes.summary: Measure max depth or node count of a tree.

worlds.hint: Deepest sub-classification under a given Dewey root.

sub_shapes.summary: Verify in-order ascending or range containment at every node.

sub_shapes.summary: Enumerate root-to-leaf paths with a value predicate on the sum.

sub_shapes.summary: Encode a tree to a string and rebuild it without loss.

sub_shapes.summary: Project the tree to a one-sided silhouette (right/left/top).

sub_shapes.summary: Trie supporting insert, exact-match, and prefix-match queries.

sub_shapes.summary: Prefix tree that returns top-k completions by recency or frequency.

worlds.hint: Top-k headline completions given a prefix.

sub_shapes.summary: Per-character trie walker checking if any inserted word matches a suffix.

worlds.hint: Sticky-pattern detector over rolling material codes.

sub_shapes.summary: Bounded min- or max-heap holding the best k seen.

worlds.hint: The k brightest targets observed tonight.

sub_shapes.summary: Top-k heap keyed by distance to a reference point.

worlds.hint: The k stops closest to the depot.

sub_shapes.summary: Min-heap over stream heads, pop-and-advance.

worlds.hint: Merge readings from many stations into one time-ordered stream.

sub_shapes.summary: Two-heap structure maintaining median as values arrive.

sub_shapes.summary: Heap-driven scheduler enforcing a cooldown per task class.

sub_shapes.summary: Count separate components via DFS/BFS or union-find.
